evals.prompt: My project has changed a lot recently and the README is outdated. The project is at D:/Project/skills-test/build-project-fixer. Please update the README to accurately reflect the current implementation.
evals.expected_output: An updated README.md that accurately describes the build-project-fixer skill's purpose, features, usage, and structure based on reading the actual source files.
expectations: The updated README mentions the project's actual purpose and features
The README accurately describes the directory structure
The README references actual files that exist in the project
No hallucinated features or files are documented

evals.prompt: Update the CHANGELOG for this project. There have been recent changes that aren't documented. The project is at D:/Project/skills-test/build-project-fixer.
evals.expected_output: An updated CHANGELOG.md (or new one if it doesn't exist) with recent changes properly categorized and described.
expectations: The CHANGELOG follows a recognized format (Keep a Changelog or similar)
Recent commits are reflected in the CHANGELOG
Changes are categorized (Added, Fixed, Changed, etc.)
Entries are written for users, not developers

evals.prompt: The docs in my project are getting stale. Can you sync all documentation (README, CHANGELOG, and docs/ folder) with the current codebase? Project at D:/Project/skills-test/agents-team-builder.
evals.expected_output: Updated documentation across README, CHANGELOG, and docs/ that accurately reflects the current codebase.
expectations: Multiple documentation files were reviewed and updated
The research phase used subagents to study the codebase
Documentation gaps were identified and addressed
No features were documented that don't exist in the code
